MOLGENIS-coreRD3

The minimal RD3 database

Overview

MOLGENIS-coreRD3 The RD3 Core is a model and database that provides the structure for capturing and managing essential individual-centric data. It defines a minimal, standardized data model focused on core information about individuals, including identifiers, demographic characteristics, and basic clinical context. In addition, the datamodel is intended as a starting point for RD3-like projects and can be extended based on the specific needs of each project. For example, projects may choose to include additional domains such as samples, clinical observations, variants, or other specialized data. This core model provides a solid foundation upon which project-specific extensions can be built. It is designed so that all connections between existing tables are preserved and function consistently. Generic tables such as processes and materials are included to support extensibility and can be used to model additional concepts, including consent, files, experiments, or variant observations, allowing projects to expand the model in a structured and interoperable way.

Data Tables

Data is stored in many tables. An overview of the primary tables is provided below or you can view all tables to find other metadata of interest. All reference tables and ontologies are stored in the ontologies database .

Table Description
Individuals An overview of the individuals (participants, patients, etc.) that are the focus of study. This includes demographics and clinical characteristics, as well as information of the data providers and references to related records within the database.
Pedigrees Overview of families (based on PED format). The definition of family members and the relationships between them are stored in Pedigree Members.
Processes A generic table used to register and describe processes or events within the system. It contains only the minimal core information required to identify and manage a process. Additional domain-specific details (e.g. clinical observations, experiments, or other process types) can be captured by extending this table with related fields or linked tables.
Experiments A generic table used to register and describe materials or entities within the system. It contains only the minimal core information required to identify and manage a material. Additional domain-specific details (e.g. samples, files, consent records, or other material types) can be captured by extending this table with related fields or linked tables.
